NCT04620733.label: Outcome
NCT04620733.source: The New England journal of medicine
NCT04620733.text: Key secondary end points were normalization of the alkaline phosphatase level at month 12 and a change in the score on the pruritus numerical rating scale (range, 0 [no itch] to 10 [worst itch imaginable]) from baseline to month 6 among patients with a baseline score of at least 4 (indicating moderate-to-severe pruritus).

NCT04526665.label: Primary
NCT04526665.source: The New England journal of medicine
NCT04526665.text: A biochemical response (the primary end point) was observed in 51% of the patients (55 of 108) who received elafibranor and in 4% (2 of 53) who received placebo, for a difference of 47 percentage points (95% confidence interval [CI], 32 to 57; P<0.001).

NCT01654731.label: Primary
NCT01654731.source: The New England journal of medicine
NCT01654731.text: RESULTS: The primary outcome occurred in 31% of the patients assigned to bezafibrate and in 0% assigned to placebo (difference, 31 percentage points; 95% confidence interval, 10 to 50; P<0.001).
